questions.question: What happens when you use the Format Painter tool in MS Word?
questions.option_1: It copies formatting (e.g., font, color) from one piece of text to another.
questions.option_3: It changes the page orientation.
questions.option_4: It inserts a hyperlink.

questions.question: What is the standard method to save your work in PowerPoint?
questions.option_1: Use the File tab and select 'Save' or press Ctrl + S
questions.option_3: Select 'Save' from the Design tab
questions.option_4: Click on the Insert tab

questions.question: What happens when you press F5 in MS PowerPoint 2019?
questions.option_1: Starts the slide show from the beginning
questions.option_3: Opens the 'Print' dialog box
questions.option_4: Inserts a new slide

questions.question: What happens when you press Ctrl + S while working on a document in computer system?
questions.option_1: Saves the current document
questions.option_3: Starts a slide show
questions.option_4: Sends the document

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the .ppsx file format in MS PowerPoint 365?
questions.option_1: To save the presentation in a format that opens directly in Slide Show mode
questions.option_2: To save the presentation as a template for future use
questions.option_3: To compress the presentation file size
questions.option_4: To save the presentation in a read-only format

questions.question: What is the main advantage of saving an MS PowerPoint presentation as a .pdf file?
questions.option_1: It allows all MS PowerPoint animations to run.
questions.option_2: It reduces file size and prevents unwanted editing.
questions.option_3: It automatically translates text into different languages.
questions.option_4: It enables users to modify slides in any PDF viewer.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Find and Replace feature (Ctrl + H) in MS Word 2019?
questions.option_1: To search for specific text and replace it with other text
questions.option_3: To change the font of the selected text
questions.option_4: To insert hyperlinks

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the 'Formula Bar' in MS Excel?
questions.option_1: To insert charts and images
questions.option_2: To display and edit the contents of a cell
questions.option_3: To navigate between worksheets
questions.option_4: To change the background color of cells

questions.question: What is the proper way to print a spreadsheet in MS Excel?
questions.option_1: Click on the 'Home' tab and select 'Print'
questions.option_3: Press Ctrl + Pt
questions.option_4: Click on the 'File' tab and choose 'Print'

questions.question: What happens when a user presses the Bullets button (•) in MS Word?
questions.option_1: The text alignment changes to center
questions.option_3: The selected text is converted into a bulleted list
questions.option_4: The selected text is converted into a numbered list

questions.question: What is the correct method to save a new workbook in MS Excel?
questions.option_1: Go to the 'Home' tab and click 'Save'
questions.option_3: Press 'Ctrl + Enter'
questions.option_4: Click the 'File' tab and choose 'Save As'

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of defining a 'Named Range' in MS Excel?
questions.option_1: To refer to a cell or range of cells using a more descriptive and easier-to-understand name in formulas
questions.option_2: To create a visual chart or graph from the selected data
questions.option_3: To apply specific formatting to a group of cells
questions.option_4: To encrypt the data within a selected range of cells

questions.question: What is the keyboard shortcut to remove paragraph indentation in MS Word 365?
questions.option_1: Ctrl + Shift + M
questions.option_3: Ctrl + M
questions.option_4: Ctrl + Shift + I

questions.question: What happens when you apply a template to an existing MS PowerPoint presentation?
questions.option_1: It only changes the background, without affecting text formatting.
questions.option_2: It completely replaces all slides and content.
questions.option_3: It applies a new design while keeping the existing slide content.
questions.option_4: It converts the PowerPoint presentation into a read-only format.

questions.question: What is the function of the keyboard shortcut Ctrl + F in Windows OS?
questions.option_1: It checks and corrects spelling errors in text.
questions.option_2: It forwards an email message to another recipient.
questions.option_3: It opens a search or find feature to locate text or items in files, webpages or applications.
questions.option_4: It formats content in a document or application.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using 'Section Breaks' in an MS Word document compared to simply using 'Page Breaks'?
questions.option_1: Section breaks automatically create a table of contents based on the headings within each section, a feature not available with standard page breaks.
questions.option_2: Section breaks allow for different page numbering schemes, headers, and footers within the same document, while page breaks only force content to the next page.
questions.option_3: Section breaks improve the document's file size and performance, especially in long documents with numerous formatting changes.
questions.option_4: Section breaks are primarily used to optimise the document for printing on different paper sizes, which is not possible with page breaks.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'AutoFill' feature in MS Excel 365?
questions.option_1: To copy cell content or continue a pattern
questions.option_2: To split text into multiple cells
questions.option_3: To validate cell data
questions.option_4: To automatically sum a range of values

questions.question: What is the main function of MS PowerPoint?
questions.option_1: Editing photos
questions.option_3: Creating and presenting slideshows
questions.option_4: Managing emails

questions.question: What happens when you press 'Enter' while working with a bullet or numbered list in MS Word 365?
questions.option_1: It converts the list into plain text.
questions.option_3: It creates a new list item with the same bullet or number.
questions.option_4: It deletes the previous bullet or number.

questions.question: What is the default file format for saving workbooks in newer versions of MS Excel (e.g., Excel 2019)?
questions.option_1: .pdf
questions.option_3: .csv
questions.option_4: .xls

questions.question: What is the purpose of Print Preview in MS Excel 365?
questions.option_1: To edit cell formulas before printing
questions.option_2: To convert the spreadsheet into a PDF
questions.option_3: To delete unnecessary rows/columns
questions.option_4: To check how the spreadsheet will appear on paper and adjust layout/formatting

questions.question: What is the significance of PowerPoint’s "Protected View", while opening a downloaded file?
questions.option_1: The presentation is encrypted and cannot be viewed
questions.option_3: It opens the file in a read-only mode, disabling most editing features
questions.option_4: The file is corrupted

questions.question: What happens if you start a new blank presentation while another presentation is already open in MS PowerPoint?
questions.option_1: MS PowerPoint displays an error message.
questions.option_2: The slides from the current presentation are copied to the new one.
questions.option_3: A new MS PowerPoint window opens with the blank presentation.
questions.option_4: The current presentation closes automatically.

questions.question: What happens if users try to open an MS Excel file created in a newer version (e.g., Excel 2021) in an older version (e.g., Excel 2010)?
questions.option_1: The file will open in Compatibility Mode with limited functionality.
questions.option_2: The file will be automatically converted to a PDF.
questions.option_3: MS Excel will display an error and refuse to open the file.
questions.option_4: The file will open normally with all features intact.

questions.question: What happens when you press 'Ctrl + M' in MS PowerPoint 365?
questions.option_1: The slide master view is opened.
questions.option_2: A new blank slide is inserted regardless of the previous layout.
questions.option_3: A new slide is inserted with the same layout as the previous slide.
questions.option_4: A duplicate of the current slide is created.
